_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize statewide-exec batch: CO LG Primavera, CT LG Bysiewicz, CT AG Tong, DE AG Jennings, DE Gov Meyer. All sitting Democratic statewide execs. Evidence-first; FALSE only where documented contrary; null where no evidence.

dianne-primavera.notes_set: CO Lt. Gov since 2019 (50th LG), second term ends Jan 2027 — TERM-LIMITED, not seeking reelection 2026. Former CO House (2007-2011, 2013-2017). LG is largely ceremonial (no legislative vote), so most cells null for lack of individual evidence. Pro-abortion advocate: member of the multi-state Reproductive Freedom Coalition; on 2025 reproductive-care bill signings said CO is 'protecting privacy, expanding access, and standing up for fundamental freedoms.' Fiscal cross-currents (opposed Gov. Ritter's property-tax freeze; sponsored small-business property-tax breaks; created an ADULT — not embryonic — stem-cell fund) don't map to the specific state-tier econ cells. 2013 CO gun-package individual roll calls could not be confirmed → null, not inferred.

0.note: Pro-abortion-rights; member of the Reproductive Freedom Coalition; promotes expanding abortion access — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Advocates expanding abortion access, the opposite of abolition.

2.note: No evidence on embryonic stem-cell/IVF discard; her stem-cell fund was for ADULT stem cells.

3.note: No public position on euthanasia/assisted suicide found.

4.note: No documented PP/NARAL/EMILY's List PAC funding found; coalition membership is not itemized funding.

0.note: No individual statement/vote on marriage definition found (LG ceremonial).

1.note: No confirmed individual vote on same-sex marriage/civil unions.

2.note: No individual statement on transgender ideology found.

4.note: No individual evidence on LGBTQ policy promotion.

0.note: No evidence on school choice/homeschool/compulsory attendance.

1.note: No evidence on parental notification/consent.
No evidence on conscience exemptions.
No evidence on voter ID.

0.note: No evidence of affirmative pro-police advocacy or defund support.

1.note: No evidence.
No evidence.
No evidence.

0.note: 2013 CO gun-package individual roll calls unconfirmed; not inferred from party.

1.note: Individual vote unconfirmed.

susan-bysiewicz.notes_set: CT Lt. Gov since 2019 (109th LG, presiding officer of the Senate), re-elected 2022; running 2026 for a third term on Gov. Lamont's ticket (won CT Dem convention endorsement May 16, 2026). Former CT House (1993-1999) and Secretary of the State (1999-2011). Textbook progressive — no cross-party deviations found. Pro-abortion: launched a 22-state Reproductive Freedom Leadership Coalition; EMILY's List + NARAL Pro-Choice CT endorsed her 2018 run. Pro-LGBTQ/trans: first to fly the Transgender Pride flag over the CT Capitol. Pro-gun-control: helped pass CT's original 1993 assault-weapons ban and urges a national AWB. Pro-sanctuary/TRUST Act. As SoS built electronic voter registration + optical-scan machines and backs no-excuse absentee voting. Family/Christian-liberty/10th-Amendment/CBDC cells null — genuine evidence gaps.

0.note: Chairs a 22-state reproductive-freedom coalition; does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Champions expanding abortion/contraception access — opposite of abolition.

4.note: Accepted EMILY's List and NARAL Pro-Choice CT endorsements (2018) — abortion-industry PAC alignment.

0.note: Celebrates 'codifying marriage equality' — does not affirm marriage as one man/one woman.

1.note: Champions same-sex marriage equality.

2.note: First to fly the Transgender Pride flag over the CT Capitol; vows to 'fight for your rights.'

4.note: Actively promotes LGBTQ policy in law and public square.

1.note: No evidence on parental notification/consent.
No evidence on conscience exemptions.

0.note: As SoS implemented optical-scan voting machines and electronic registration — supports machines, not hand-counted paper ballots.

1.note: No direct statement on photo voter ID.

2.note: Backs no-excuse absentee + early voting — opposite of single-day in-person.

0.note: Supported 2020 police-accountability reform; no affirmative pro-police advocacy or defund support — null.

1.note: Transition-team bail-reform group is administration-level/SOFT — null.
No evidence (posture is blue-state resistance, not state-sovereignty conservatism).
No evidence on mandatory deportation.

2.note: Pro-sanctuary/TRUST Act; opposes rolling it back.

0.note: Strong gun-control advocate; helped pass CT's assault-weapons ban — opposes permitless carry.

1.note: Urges national assault-weapons ban using CT's law as a model.

2.note: No direct statement on NFA/GCA repeal.

william-tong.notes_set: CT AG since 2019 (25th AG), running 2026 for a third term (not for governor); elected 2026 NAAG President. Former CT House (2007-2019, chaired Judiciary). Abundant HARD evidence via multistate amicus briefs/lawsuits — uniformly progressive, no cross-party deviations. Defends medication abortion/mifepristone access (multiple SCOTUS briefs). Pro-trans: briefs defending gender-affirming care for minors and trans students. In Foote v. Ludlow argued schools may keep a student's gender identity from parents (against parental notification). Defended eliminating CT's religious exemption to vaccine mandates. Sued to block Trump's proof-of-citizenship voting EO. Defends CT's assault-weapons/magazine ban; criticized Bruen. Defends CT sanctuary/TRUST Act; threatened to be 'first to sue' over birthright-citizenship rollback.

0.note: Multiple amicus briefs defending abortion access; runs a standing AG 'Reproductive Rights' initiative.

1.note: Fights abortion restrictions — opposite of abolition.

4.note: NARAL alignment is SOFT (thanked, not formal endorsement); no documented PAC funding.

1.note: No same-sex-marriage-specific position found.

2.note: Filed briefs defending gender-affirming care for minors and transgender students — does not affirm immutable biological sex.

4.note: Promotes LGBTQ legal protections in employment, schools, healthcare.

1.note: In Foote v. Ludlow argued schools may keep a student's gender identity hidden from parents — against parental notification.
Defended/praised eliminating CT's religious exemption to vaccine mandates — against conscience exemptions.

4.note: Big-tech antitrust enforcement present, but no anti-WEF/ESG/Davos evidence — compound cell null.

1.note: Sued to block proof-of-citizenship voter-registration EO; called such restrictions unconstitutional — against photo-ID/citizenship-verification regime.

0.note: No affirmative pro-police advocacy or defund support.

1.note: Authored CT bail-reform statute as a legislator (pre-AG) — SOFT/null.

0.note: No conservative-direction nullification; federal resistance is left-flavored.

1.note: Federal-litigation posture targets GOP policy — not the cell's conservative intent.

2.note: No conservative-direction resistance on guns/life/religious liberty (did the opposite).

1.note: Opposed deportation efforts (Haitian TPS).

4.note: Threatened to be 'first to sue' to defend birthright citizenship — against opposing it.

0.note: Defends CT's assault-weapons/magazine ban; criticized Bruen — opposes permitless carry.

1.note: Vigorously defends the assault-weapons and magazine ban.

kathy-jennings.notes_set: DE AG since 2019 (46th AG), reelected 2022, running 2026 for a third term (first declared candidate, Feb 2025). Abundant HARD evidence via multistate briefs/lawsuits — consistent progressive, no cross-party deviations. Pro-abortion: amicus briefs defending abortion access and mifepristone; runs a free abortion legal helpline. Pro-trans: sued HHS and Trump admin to protect gender-affirming care. Progressive prosecutor: reduced mandatory minimums, diversion over incarceration, supports risk-based (not cash) bail. Defended DE vote-by-mail/same-day-registration and sued over Trump voting-restriction EOs. Defends DE's assault-weapons/magazine and ghost-gun bans ('third win over the gun lobby'). Sued to block Trump's birthright-citizenship EO and immigration-enforcement funding conditions.

0.note: Joined amicus briefs defending abortion access; 'a woman's right to choose is settled law.'

1.note: Defends mifepristone/abortion access — opposite of abolition.

3.note: No position found on DE's End-of-Life Options Act.

4.note: No documented PP/NARAL PAC funding found.

1.note: No same-sex-marriage-specific brief found.

2.note: Sued HHS and Trump admin to protect gender-affirming care — does not affirm immutable biological sex.

4.note: Promotes LGBTQ/trans protections in policy and law.

1.note: No direct Jennings action on parental notification (DE Reg 225 is a Board policy, not hers).

2.note: No direct evidence on CRT/SOGI/sex-ed curriculum.

1.note: No religious-conscience brief found.

4.note: Consumer-protection/antitrust enforcement present, but no anti-WEF/ESG evidence — compound cell null.

1.note: Sued over voting restrictions and defended vote-by-mail — against the photo-ID/citizenship-verification regime.

2.note: Defended early voting + same-day registration — opposite of single-day in-person.

0.note: Reform-oriented; no affirmative pro-police advocacy or defund support.

1.note: Progressive prosecutor: reduced mandatory minimums, diversion over incarceration, supports risk-based (not cash) bail.

0.note: Anti-federal litigation is exclusively anti-Trump/left-flavored — not the cell's conservative intent.

1.note: No conservative-direction evidence.

2.note: No conservative-direction resistance on guns/life/religious liberty.

1.note: Issued immigrant 'Know Your Rights' guidance; opposes federal enforcement leverage.

2.note: Sued over immigration-enforcement conditions; pro-immigrant — does not oppose sanctuary policy.

4.note: Sued to block Trump's birthright-citizenship EO — defends birthright citizenship.

0.note: Defends DE's assault-weapons and large-capacity-magazine ban — opposes permitless carry.

1.note: Defends the AWB/mag ban and ghost-gun ban ('third win over the gun lobby').

matt-meyer.notes_set: DE Governor since Jan 21, 2025 (76th Gov); term to Jan 2029, next gubernatorial election 2028 (no 2028 filing yet). Former New Castle County Executive (2017-2025). Fast-accumulating HARD record of bills signed/EOs — consistently progressive, with one genuine cross-current on public safety. SIGNED the Medical Aid in Dying Act (HB140) his predecessor had vetoed; signed HB205 abortion/IVF/gender-affirming-care shield law; joined the Reproductive Freedom Alliance; PP Advocacy Fund of DE endorsed his 2024 run. Signed EO#11 protecting gender-affirming care (incl. minors) and EO#20 expanding the LGBTQ+ Commission; backs marriage-equality amendment. Signed a FY26 budget adding new top income-tax brackets. Signed anti-ICE bills (HB182 bars 287(g) agreements) and EO#9 (Office of Gun Violence Prevention). CROSS-CURRENT: signed pro-law-enforcement bills (SB38 retired police-animal care, SB112 academy naming) and HB123 (new felony for discharging firearms at homes/businesses/places of worship) — documented credit on public-justice.

0.note: Joined the Reproductive Freedom Alliance: 'Delaware will always stand on the side of reproductive freedom.'

1.note: Signed HB205 abortion-shield law; backs a reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment.

2.note: HB205 also shields IVF/fertility services — supports IVF, contrary to opposing embryo discard.

3.note: Signed the Medical Aid in Dying Act (HB140) his predecessor had vetoed — legalized physician-assisted suicide.

4.note: Endorsed by the Planned Parenthood Advocacy Fund of Delaware (2024).

0.note: Supports the SB100 marriage-equality constitutional amendment — does not affirm one-man/one-woman marriage.

1.note: Backs enshrining same-sex marriage in the DE constitution.

2.note: Signed EO#11 protecting gender-affirming care including for minors.

4.note: Signed EO#20 expanding the DE LGBTQ+ Commission; EO#11 — promotes LGBTQ policy.

0.note: Focus is public-school funding equity; no documented support or opposition to school choice.

1.note: EO#11 shields minor gender care from out-of-state probes but does not directly address in-state parental consent — null.

3.note: DE's porn age-verification bill failed; no Meyer signing or position.

1.note: No documented conscience-exemption position (carve-out absence is SOFT inference — null).

4.note: EO#15 expands an interfaith council, not Christian-specific — does not map.

2.note: Signed a (constitutionally required) balanced budget; tax increases don't map to deficit/BBA — null.

1.note: No signed voter-ID measure or direct position.

2.note: Mail-in/early-voting amendments don't require his signature; no signed bill — null.

3.note: No HARD signing on mail-in/drop boxes — null.

0.note: Signed pro-law-enforcement bills (SB38 retired police-animal care, SB112 State Police Academy naming) — affirmative support for law enforcement, not defund.

1.note: No evidence on cashless bail/rogue prosecutors.

2.note: Signed HB123 creating a new felony for discharging firearms at occupied homes, businesses, vehicles, or places of worship — proportional punishment of violent crime.

0.note: State-sovereignty posture runs against the Trump federal govt, not conservative federalism — null.

1.note: No conservative-direction evidence.

2.note: No conservative-direction resistance on guns/life/religious liberty.

1.note: Signed anti-ICE bills limiting immigration enforcement — opposite of mandatory deportation.

2.note: Signed HB182 barring 287(g) ICE agreements — supports sanctuary-type policy.

0.note: Signed EO#9 establishing an Office of Gun Violence Prevention — gun-control posture, opposes permitless carry.

1.note: Gun-control governing posture (gun-violence-prevention office).
